symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive solid-to-liquid dysphagia over months, now unable to swallow liquids
symptoms.onset: insidious over ~4 months, abrupt worsening over 48 hours
symptoms.character: mechanical food sticking progressing to inability to pass liquids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stepwise solids-then-liquids progression is characteristic of a growing mechanical obstruction rather than a motility disorder.
Achalasia classically causes dysphagia equal for solids and liquids from onset, not a stepwise progression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound unintentional weight loss (~15 kg in 3 months) with cachexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid unintentional weight loss is a cardinal malignancy alarm feature and argues strongly against a benign stricture.
A benign scar does not usually produce this degree of rapid wasting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coughing and choking immediately on swallowing liquids (aspiration)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Overflow of pooled liquid into the larynx indicates near-total obstruction and active aspiration risk.
Immediate coughing on swallowing liquids can reflect a fistulous communication with the airway.
Painful swallowing accompanies mucosal ulceration from an obstructing lesion.
Candidiasis can cause odynophagia and may coexist, but does not explain mechanical obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regurgitation of foul-smelling undigested retained food

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Food retained and fermenting above a high-grade obstruction is regurgitated undigested and malodorous.
Hoarseness suggests recurrent laryngeal nerve involvement by locally advanced tumour.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to maintain oral hydration with weakness / bedbound state (dehydration proxy)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Total inability to take oral fluids with marked weakness indicates dehydration requiring IV resuscitation.
Smoking is a major risk factor for oesophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
Chronic reflux predisposes to Barrett's oesophagus and adenocarcinoma.
Chronic acid injury can cause benign scarring and stricture — the important-not-to-miss benign mimic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm fixed left supraclavicular (Virchow's) lymph node

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Troisier sign — a metastatic supraclavicular node strongly indicates advanced abdominal/thoracic malignancy; sealed clinician palpation finding, not obtainable via telehealth.
Auscultatory crackles localise the aspiration; sealed clinician exam finding not available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension (BP 90/60) with tachycardia (HR 110), low-grade fever (37.6), mild hypoxia (SpO2 94%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low blood pressure with compensatory tachycardia indicates volume depletion; sealed clinician-measured vitals — the AI Doctor infers circulatory compromise from the history of no oral intake and weakness, not from a measured number.
Fever and mild hypoxia are consistent with an early aspiration chest infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper GI endoscopy with biopsy visualising an irregular obstructing mass

investigation_details.expected_result: Irregular obstructing oesophageal mass; histology for tissue diagnosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: No tissue diagnosis or staging; the definitive test is hospital-based and cannot be performed via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Endoscopy with biopsy is the definitive diagnostic and cannot be substituted by remote assessment.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Progressive dysphagia + rapid weight loss + hoarseness + a metastatic node together strongly indicate advanced oesophageal malignancy and mandate urgent endoscopy/specialist referral.
Choking on liquids with an inability to hydrate, chest crackles and hypotension is the acute emergency that drives an ambulance-today disposition.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital assessment with IV rehydration and chest assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Active aspiration, inability to hydrate and dehydration require in-person emergency care today.
Definitive diagnosis and staging of the obstructing lesion; part of a suspected-cancer (2-week-wait) pathway, arranged in hospital.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest imaging (CXR) and bloods (FBC, U&E, LFTs, albumin)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess aspiration pneumonitis, dehydration and nutritional status — hospital investigations, not obtainable via telehealth.
